tiers.name: small
tiers.goal: trusted anchor tasks with direct hidden-oracle benches or formal collateral

tiers.name: medium
tiers.goal: single-IP manuals and protocol slices that still fit into one verification episode
tiers.acquisition: prefer official PDF docs; otherwise render authoritative HTML docs to PDF during ingest

tiers.name: large
tiers.goal: subsystem, core, controller, and SoC-level manuals with richer integration constraints
tiers.acquisition: repository-linked user manuals, top-level datasheets, and open protocol standards

tiers.name: industrial
tiers.goal: real standards and SoC architecture packages that require retrieval, chunking, and legal review
tiers.acquisition: treat public and license-gated sources separately

datasets.name: rtllm_v1_1
datasets.notes: Small hand-written designs with explicit descriptions, a hidden simulation bench, and a verified RTL reference.

datasets.name: verilogeval_v2_spec_to_rtl
datasets.notes: Spec-to-RTL benchmark with the improved VerilogEval v2 harness and structured failure analysis.

datasets.name: rtllm_v2_0
datasets.notes: Expanded RTLLM benchmark with categorized tasks; good for widening anchor-task families before scaling.

datasets.name: asserteval
datasets.notes: Use this to train assertion and formal-task generation, not as the main generator benchmark. The current AssertLLM repo README says 20 designs; the later OpenLLM-RTL paper summary says 18, so verify the checkout you actually use.

datasets.name: chipbench
datasets.notes: ChipBench Verilog generation subset (30 self-contained + 6 non-self-contained + 9 CPU IP). Uses VerilogEval-style TopModule/RefModule harness. Upstream uses iverilog v12-branch. Oracle uses verilator (42/45) + xrun fallback for BLKANDNBLK tasks (2/3). 44/45 gold selftests pass. 1 failure: Prob006_cpu_top uses '{default:0} which neither iverilog v12 nor verilator supports.

datasets.name: realbench
datasets.notes: Real IP cores (AES 6, SD card 14, E203 RISC-V 40) with markdown specs, testbenches, and formal equiv checking. Specs GPG-encrypted (passphrase: iprc-dip). Upstream uses verilator via per-task Makefiles. 56/60 gold selftests pass. 4 SDC failures: 2 upstream syntax bugs ($random[7:0]), 1 verilator version issue (VL_URANDOM_RANGE_Q), 1 passes with upstream Makefile but not our oracle (sd_data_master BD_EMPTY defined inside gold RTL).

datasets.name: resbench
datasets.notes: 56 FPGA resource-aware problems across 12 categories (combinational, FSM, crypto, ML, physics, etc). Self-checking testbenches without gold RTL. All 56 oracles compile and run correctly with iverilog. No gold RTL provided by benchmark; tested with GPT-4o solutions (51 pass) and generator agent (2 more pass).

datasets.name: icrtl
datasets.notes: 6 industry-scale design challenges from IC design contests (LBP, GEMM, 2D-conv, Huffman coding, job assignment, distance transform). All use TOP as the DUT module name. Testbenches use data files via ./00_TB/ relative paths. 4/6 gold selftests pass; conv/dt ref solutions are incomplete starting-point files (initial.sv), not passing gold RTL. Oracles/testbenches work correctly. From EvolVE (arXiv 2601.18067).

datasets.name: verilog_axi
datasets.notes: AXI4/AXI4-Lite bus components (adapters, crossbars, DMA, FIFOs, RAMs, registers). Cocotb testbenches using cocotbext-axi. Oracle runs upstream Makefile with icarus.

datasets.name: verilog_ethernet
datasets.notes: Ethernet MAC/PHY/UDP/IP stack components (1G/10G/25G). Cocotb testbenches using cocotbext-eth. Oracle runs upstream Makefile with icarus.

datasets.name: verilog_pcie
datasets.notes: PCIe DMA, TLP, MSI-X, configuration space components. Cocotb testbenches using cocotbext-pcie. Oracle runs upstream Makefile with icarus.

datasets.name: verilog_uart
datasets.notes: UART RX and TX modules. Cocotb testbenches using cocotbext-uart. 2/2 oracle pass.

datasets.name: verilog_axis
datasets.notes: AXI-Stream infrastructure components (adapters, FIFOs, muxes, demuxes, switches, rate limiters, cobs codec). Cocotb testbenches using cocotbext-axi.

datasets.name: verilog_lfsr
datasets.notes: LFSR, CRC, PRBS generators/checkers, scramble/descramble modules. Cocotb testbenches.

datasets.name: pulp_common_cells
datasets.notes: ETH Zurich PULP Platform common building blocks: CDC, clock dividers, FIFOs, arbiters, stream crossbars, ECC, address decoders. SystemVerilog assert-based TBs. Oracle uses xrun.

datasets.name: veer_el2
datasets.notes: VeeR EL2 RISC-V core block-level components: PIC, DMA, ALU, multiplier, divider, decoder, PMP, IFU, LSU, AHB/AXI bridges. Production-quality cocotb+pyuvm testbenches from CHIPS Alliance. Oracle runs upstream Makefile with verilator.

datasets.name: riscv_hardware_specs
datasets.notes: First spec-only curated pack checked into the repo as official RISC-V hardware/control PDFs. The active bundle materializes bounded IMSIC / APLIC IDC tasks carved from the Advanced Interrupt Architecture v1.0 PDF as public-only task artifacts so the trainer can validate a no-oracle ingestion path before adding larger spec corpora. External Debug remains checked in only as raw source material for future standalone RTL tasks.

datasets.name: opentitan_dv
datasets.notes: Repository-native industrial verification artifacts for later agentic verification benchmarks.

datasets.name: opentitan
datasets.notes: First curated pack is checked into the repo as manually ingested OpenTitan docs for adc_ctrl, aon_timer, uart, i2c, spi_host, pattgen, dma, rv_timer, and sysrst_ctrl from the local checkout. The public task metadata is curated, the spec content is copied from upstream docs, and the materialized task store references a shared hidden source bundle for upstream rtl/dv paths needed by future oracle work. GPIO was not present in this checkout, so sysrst_ctrl remains in the pack.

datasets.name: openhw_core_ip_manuals
datasets.notes: Use focused OpenHW manuals or manual chapters, especially CORE-V single-core manuals, as medium-sized architecture specs. These are a good bridge from toy tasks to real manuals because they already point at mature RTL and verification repos.

datasets.name: wishbone_b4
datasets.notes: Use Wishbone B4 as an open protocol-spec family. It is useful for medium-tier interface tasks once paired with OpenCores or LiteX-compatible implementations and hand-built oracle benches.

datasets.name: opentitan_top_docs
datasets.notes: Use OpenTitan top-level datasheets such as Earl Grey and Darjeeling for large-tier SoC-style specifications. These give a real integration story while staying fully public and repo-linked.

datasets.name: cva6_user_manual
datasets.notes: Use the CVA6 user manual family as a large public CPU-core corpus. It is large enough to force retrieval/chunking, but still connected to public RTL and verification infrastructure.

datasets.name: core_v_mcu_user_manual
fields: user manual PDF or rendered HTML
datasets.notes: Use CORE-V-MCU as a public large-tier SoC manual with multiple peripherals, DMA, and subsystem integration concerns.

datasets.name: nvdla_docs
datasets.notes: Use NVDLA as a large public accelerator-scale corpus. The docs are paired with RTL, a reference testbench, sanity tests, and synthesis material, which makes it unusually useful for verifier-agent work.

datasets.name: onfi_specs
datasets.notes: Use ONFI protocol specs as the open large-tier analogue to DDR-style memory-interface work. They are downloadable public PDFs and cover high-speed NAND interfaces with real timing/protocol complexity.

datasets.name: rtl_repo
datasets.notes: Repository-level scale corpus for context conditioning and code exposure, not a trustworthy reward oracle.

datasets.name: jedec_ddr3_private
datasets.notes: Use this only once legal access is settled. DDR3-class standards are exactly the kind of industrial corpora you want, but they should sit in a separate private pipeline, not the public dataset path.

datasets.name: jedec_ddr6_private
datasets.notes: Treat DDR6 and similar next-generation standards as a separate industrial-private tier. They are valuable long-term targets, but not good public bootstrap data because the standards and collateral are access-controlled.
